name: Exam Pack Sample
description: 20 challenging exam-style questions to test your knowledge.

items.promptText: What does 'HTTP' stand for?
items.explanationText: HyperText Transfer Protocol is the foundation of data communication on the web. HTTPS adds TLS encryption on top. HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 are faster modern versions.

items.promptText: What is the pH of pure water at 25°C?
items.explanationText: Pure water has a pH of exactly 7 — perfectly neutral. The scale runs from 0 (most acidic) to 14 (most alkaline). A pH below 7 is acidic; above 7 is basic.

items.promptText: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
items.explanationText: The mitochondrion produces ATP via cellular respiration, supplying energy for most cellular processes. Cells with high energy demands — muscle cells, neurons — contain thousands of mitochondria.

items.promptText: What is the SI unit of electric resistance?
items.explanationText: The ohm (Ω) is the SI unit of electrical resistance, named after Georg Simon Ohm. Ohm's Law: V = IR. One ohm equals one volt per ampere (1 Ω = 1 V/A).

items.promptText: In what year did World War I begin?
items.explanationText: WWI began in 1914, triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria-Hungary. The war ended in 1918, killing approximately 17 million people across Europe and beyond.

items.promptText: What does 'GDP' stand for?
items.explanationText: Gross Domestic Product measures the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country in a given period. It is the most widely used indicator of economic output.

items.promptText: How many planets are in our Solar System (IAU definition, 2006)?
items.explanationText: In 2006, the IAU redefined 'planet', demoting Pluto to 'dwarf planet'. The 8 planets are: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.

items.promptText: What does acceleration measure?
items.explanationText: Acceleration measures how quickly velocity changes. It can describe speeding up, slowing down, or changing direction.

items.promptText: Who developed the theory of classical conditioning?
items.explanationText: Ivan Pavlov discovered classical conditioning while studying digestion in dogs. Dogs salivated at a bell paired with food — an involuntary conditioned reflex. This work laid the groundwork for behaviorism.

items.promptText: Approximately how many languages are currently spoken in the world?
items.explanationText: About 7,000 languages are spoken globally, though the number is declining rapidly. Around 40% are considered endangered. The top 23 languages account for more than half the world's speakers.

items.promptText: What does 'SQL' stand for?
items.explanationText: Structured Query Language is the standard language for relational databases, supporting CRUD operations. First developed at IBM in the 1970s, SQL remains one of the most widely used languages today.

items.promptText: How many pairs of chromosomes do humans typically have?
items.explanationText: Humans have 23 pairs of chromosomes (46 total) in each somatic cell. 22 pairs are autosomes; the 23rd pair determines biological sex (XX = female, XY = male). Trisomy of chromosome 21 causes Down syndrome.

items.promptText: What is the standard gravitational acceleration on Earth's surface?
items.explanationText: The standard gravitational acceleration is 9.81 m/s² (often rounded to 9.8 or 10 m/s²). It varies slightly by location — stronger at the poles than the equator due to Earth's rotation and non-uniform mass distribution.

items.promptText: What is the closest star to Earth other than the Sun?
items.explanationText: Proxima Centauri is a red dwarf 4.24 light-years from Earth, part of the Alpha Centauri triple system. It hosts a potentially habitable planet, Proxima b.

items.promptText: Who was the first human to walk on the Moon?
items.explanationText: Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969, during NASA's Apollo 11 mission. His words: 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.' Buzz Aldrin joined him shortly after.

items.promptText: What bias makes people favor evidence that supports what they already believe?
items.explanationText: Confirmation bias makes people notice and remember supporting evidence more than conflicting evidence. It can distort judgment and research.

items.promptText: What does opportunity cost mean in economics?
items.explanationText: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best option you give up when making a choice. Every decision has a tradeoff.
options: The value of the next best alternative given up
The price rise across an economy
The money a company earns after tax

items.promptText: Which subatomic particle carries no electric charge?
items.explanationText: The neutron is found in the atomic nucleus with no electric charge. Protons carry +1 charge, electrons carry -1. The number of neutrons determines the isotope of an element.

items.promptText: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
items.explanationText: Gold's symbol Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'. Atomic number 79. Gold is valued for its rarity, malleability, and corrosion resistance — it does not tarnish or react with most acids.

items.promptText: What does 'LLM' stand for in the context of artificial intelligence?
items.explanationText: Large Language Model refers to AI models trained on vast text datasets using transformer architectures. Examples: GPT-4, Claude, Gemini. They excel at text generation, summarisation, and reasoning tasks.

title: Exam Pack Sample
